summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that YAML.load without safe loading allows arbitrary Ruby object deserialization, which can lead to code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that arbitrary Ruby objects can be deserialized through YAML.load, creating a proof file that would not exist under normal operation.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully deserialized an arbitrary Ruby object (OpenStruct) from the malicious YAML file and executed code, creating the proof file and confirming the vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script creates a malicious YAML file with a Ruby object, loads it using YAML.load, and checks for the creation of a proof file. This distinguishes successful deserialization from normal YAML parsing.
evidence_produced_by_run: The malicious YAML file containing a Ruby object was successfully loaded
Arbitrary Ruby object (OpenStruct) was instantiated during deserialization
Proof file was created, demonstrating code execution through deserialization
This confirms that YAML.load can be exploited for remote code execution

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the existing target repository at /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_e7debecf/repo mounted read-only at /target-repo
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: current state of repo directory (not a git repository)
